task_completion.description: Verify the agent generated a 3D surface of the bone mask with at least 10,000 vertices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius UI showing the 3D skull surface. The surface properties or details panel should display a vertex count of 10,000 or more.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent exported the 3D surface as a VRML 2.0 file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius export dialog saving the file as 'legacy_skull.wrl' in '/home/ga/Documents/', or a file manager/terminal showing this file exists.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent created the mesh_info.txt file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the creation or existence of '/home/ga/Documents/mesh_info.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify the vertex count recorded in the text file matches the actual vertex count of the generated surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The integer written in 'mesh_info.txt' exactly matches (or is within 1% of) the vertex count displayed in the InVesalius UI for the generated 3D surface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used InVesalius to generate the 3D surface and export the VRML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the InVesalius 3 interface, including the segmentation and 3D surface generation panels.

integrity.description: Verify the agent obtained the vertex count from the software rather than hallucinating a number.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen viewing the surface properties/details in InVesalius to read the vertex count before writing it to the text file.

integrity.description: Verify the VRML file was exported via the application and not created manually as a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export process should be visible in InVesalius (e.g., 'Export 3D surface' dialog), rather than terminal commands like 'touch legacy_skull.wrl'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The VRML output path must be '/home/ga/Documents/legacy_skull.wrl'. The info output path must be '/home/ga/Documents/mesh_info.txt'. The generated 3D surface must have a minimum of 10,000 vertices to be considered a bona fide skull mesh. The vertex count recorded in the text file must match the actual vertex count of the generated mesh within a 1.0% tolerance.
